channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: GAO: Wind Risk Linked to Larger Insurance Premium Jumps Than Wildfire
item.description: A new report from the Government Accountability Office finds that average premium increases between risk levels are larger for wind than for wildfire. Premiums in areas at severe or extreme risk for wind and wildfire also grew faster than premiums in areas at major risk, the office reported. Between 2019 and 2024, homes in areas classified as severe or extreme wind risk had premiums about 58% higher on average—approximately $1,294 more per year—than similar homes in major wind risk areas, the next lower category.

item.title: U.S., Iran Agree to Two-Week Ceasefire Brokered by Pakistan
item.description: The United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan, potentially suspending a six-week-old war that has killed thousands, spread across the Middle East and caused unprecedented disruption to the world’s energy supplies. The ceasefire suspends the war launched on February 28 by Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who had said at the time that they sought to prevent Iran from projecting force beyond its borders, end its nuclear program and create conditions for Iranians to topple their rulers. T rump announced the agreement late on Tuesday, just two hours before a deadline he had set for Iran to open the blockaded Strait of Hormuz or face the destruction of its “whole civilisation.” Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said he had invited Iranian and U.S.

item.title: ARPC cyclone reinsurance pool claim payments surpass AU$1bn
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Tarun Mathur resigns as Policybazaar Insurance Brokers CEO, replaced by Sajja Praveen Chowdary
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: JKL Partners to relaunch Lotte Non-life sale: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA imposes restrictions on Bazar Money Transfer Limited
item.description: On 21 November 2025, we imposed restrictions on Bazar Money Transfer Limited (BMTL), preventing it from providing regulated payment services. BMTL is registered with the FCA to provide money remittance services to retail and corporate customers. As BMTL was no longer meeting the conditions for registration as a small payment institution, we acted to impose restrictions to protect consumers, preventing BMTL from carrying out any regulated payment services.

item.title: Most Active And Highest-Spending Startup Investors Diverged In Q1
item.description: The investors backing the highest number of startup rounds this past quarter were mostly not the ones writing the biggest checks. For Q1, the most active lead investors in post-seed rounds were Accel, Andreessen Horowitz, and Lightspeed Venture Partners . Of course, the most active lead investors aren’t always the ones writing the biggest checks.

item.title: Florida Peninsula lifts Palm Re 2026-1 cat bond target to $225m, at lower pricing
item.description: Florida Peninsula came back to the cat bond market in March , for what will be the company’s third sponsorship in the Palm Re series of deals, with an initial target to secure $150 million of indemnity based Florida named storm reinsurance from this offering of notes. As we’ve explained before, this new Palm Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond will sit higher up in the Florida Peninsula reinsurance tower, with the 2025 and 2024 notes both in layers beneath it. It appears that Florida Peninsula will look to secure more than its initially targeted reinsurance from its latest catastrophe bond, and at lower pricing than the initial guidance.

item.title: UCITS catastrophe bond funds surpassed milestone $20bn in AUM in Q1 2026
item.description: Catastrophe bond funds in the UCITS format continued to expand their combined assets under management (AUM) through the first-quarter, surpassing the milestone $20 billion in February before declining slightly to just under $19.8 billion by the end of March 2026. That marks the first time the UCITS cat bond fund sector has surpassed $20 billion in assets across the group of fund strategies, a new milestone and one that serves to demonstrate the importance of these funds to the catastrophe bond marketplace. Catastrophe bond funds grew strongly through 2025, adding roughly $5.3 billion or 39% across the asset base of the UCITS structured cat bond fund strategies .

item.title: Cat bond prices drop 20%+ YoY, investors willing to support riskier tranches: Gallagher Securities
item.description: Catastrophe bond pricing has fallen by more than 20% year-on-year as of early March 2026, according to Gallagher Securities, with the company explaining that the softer pricing environment means that investors have been willing to allocate capital to riskier tranches of cat bond notes. Gallagher Securities explained, “With the softening market, we have seen investors willing to invest in riskier tranches to maintain absolute return levels for their funds.” Which does not seem so much about meeting target returns (expected return fund targets are usually in the single digits anyway), as perhaps sustaining returns nearer to the levels which have attracted a lot of new capital to the catastrophe bond asset class in recent years. “With the softening market, investors are willing to invest in riskier tranches, with roughly 18% of Q1 2026 issuance volume covering risks with expected losses below the 1-in-25-year return period.” The Gallagher Securities team further stated, “As of early March, cat bond pricing was down over 20% year-over-year.

item.title: Gen Re appoints Daniel Osterrieder as Head of National/Multinational Treaty in North America
item.description: Gen Re, a reinsurance company and a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway, has announced the appointment of Daniel Osterrieder as Head of National/Multinational Treaty in North America. Osterrieder brings extensive leadership experience, a well-established network of client relationships, and significant underwriting expertise gained across the industry. His background positions him to support Gen Re’s objectives and strengthen its presence in the North American market.

item.title: Indian April 1 renewal one of the most competitive in recent years: Guy Carpenter
item.description: Adding: “This renewal has seen an extension of the softening trend seen at January 1, with improved terms, increased competition and greater flexibility across most Indian reinsurance programs, making it one of the most competitive renewal seasons seen in recent years.” The April 1 renewal period saw notable pricing movements across various lines in India. India’s insurers secured some of the most favourable reinsurance terms in recent history during the April 1 renewals, with risk-adjusted rates declining by as much as 30%, reports reinsurance broker Guy Carpenter. Driven by a surge in local capacity and a relatively benign loss year, this renewal season marked a definitive softening of the market, offering more flexibility for domestic players, according to Guy Carpenter’s April 1, 2026 Reinsurance Renewal Report.

item.title: ARPC cyclone reinsurance pool claim payments exceed $1bn
item.description: Australian Reinsurance Pool Corporation (ARPC) has reached a major milestone, with total cyclone reinsurance pool claim payments now exceeding $1 billion since the scheme commenced. The Terrorism Reinsurance Pool commenced operations on 1st July 2003, and the Cyclone Reinsurance Pool commenced operations on 1st July 2022. To date, ARPC has made claim payments relating to 20 declared cyclones and supported residential, small and medium enterprises (SMEs), and strata policyholders across northern Australia.

item.title: UBS Loses Bid to Limit Liability for Nazi-Linked Crimes, Judge’s Ruling Shows
item.description: judge on Tuesday rejected UBS’ bid to clarify a $1.25 billion settlement from 1999 of Holocaust-related litigation against Swiss banks by shielding it from claims based on new revelations about Nazi-linked accounts. District Judge Edward Korman in Brooklyn, New York, said UBS was seeking an advisory opinion protecting it from “hypothetical” lawsuits that have not been filed. UBS sought Korman’s intervention after an investigation commissioned in 2020 by the former Credit Suisse uncovered additional ties between that bank, its predecessors and Nazis, including 890 accounts with potential Nazi links.

item.title: Marsh expands Cyber ECHO to $200m with enhanced flexibility and client support
item.description: By integrating enhanced protection with risk management support, Marsh is enabling organisations to approach cyber risk with greater confidence and preparedness. Marsh has expanded its Cyber ECHO facility to $200 million, further strengthening one of the industry’s most comprehensive cyber insurance solutions and enhancing protection for clients worldwide. Originally launched in 2016, Cyber ECHO has evolved into a leading global excess cyber risk facility, bringing together capacity from insurers across the Lloyd’s, company markets, and Bermuda.

item.title: Liberty Mutual appoints Vlad Barbalat to lead unified risk and capital strategy across GRS and LMI
item.description: The post Liberty Mutual appoints Vlad Barbalat to lead unified risk and capital strategy across GRS and LMI appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Liberty Mutual has introduced a new leadership structure spanning its Global Risk Solutions (GRS) and Liberty Mutual Investments (LMI) businesses, aiming to better align risk, capital, and strategic execution. At the centre of the restructure, Vlad Barbalat has been appointed president of Global Risk & Capital Solutions, in addition to his current role as chief investment officer of the group.

item.title: Compare the Market brings ChatGPT integration to insurance pricing journey with new car cover tool
item.description: The post Compare the Market brings ChatGPT integration to insurance pricing journey with new car cover tool appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Compare the Market has launched a ChatGPT integration that allows users to estimate car insurance costs through a conversational interface, marking a further step in the embedding of generative AI within insurance distribution journeys. Positioned as a pre-quote experience, the integration keeps users within ChatGPT while supporting early-stage comparison of insurance costs.

item.title: PCMS adds AI underwriting to surety platform
item.description: Provider of P&C enterprise solutions for small to mid-sized carriers PCMS has introduced AI-augmented underwriting capabilities to its Atlas for Surety platform, aimed at improving efficiency in financial and contract risk analysis. The platform is designed to reduce manual data entry and accelerate underwriting timelines without changing underwriting practices. “Surety underwriting depends on judgment, consistency, and deep financial understanding.

item.title: Substance AI seeks $2 million in new offering
item.description: Substance AI , a startup focused on AI-driven insurance workflows, is raising up to $2 million in a new offering, according to a recent SEC filing . The company has sold $50,000 to date from two investors, with $1.95 million remaining in the round. The offering is being made under Rule 506(b).

item.title: Munich Re Ventures backs Starfish Space $100 million round
item.description: Munich Re Ventures participated in a $100 million+ Series B round for Starfish Space , which was led by Point72 Ventures . Other investors in the round include Activate Capital, Shield Capital, Industrious Ventures, NightDragon, NFX, Toyota Ventures, PSL Ventures, Nomi Capital, Gaingels, and Overlap Holdings. Seattle-based Starfish Space develops satellite servicing technology through its Otter vehicle, supporting missions such as life extension for geostationary satellites and disposal for low Earth orbit assets.

item.title: Doctor i lanza la primera IA que recomienda seguros de salud con precios reales
item.description: Mientras que herramientas genéricas de inteligencia artificial ofrecen respuestas generales, Doctor i IA está entrenada con los datos reales del comparador, referente en España en la comparación de seguros de salud con más de 1,2 millones de asesoramientos al año. Doctor i presenta el primer asistente de inteligencia artificial especializado en seguros médicos en España , diseñado para ayudar a los usuarios a entender, comparar y elegir su seguro de salud de forma inmediata y personalizada. La inteligencia artificial está transformando múltiples industrias, y ahora también llega al mundo de los seguros de salud.

item.title: Luz verde al marco proporcional para aseguradoras pequeñas en Europa
item.description: Eiopa ha publicado las especificaciones técnicas dirigidas a identificar a las entidades aseguradoras y grupos pequeños y no complejos (SNCUs y SNCGs), en el marco de la revisión de la Directiva de Solvencia II. Con esta iniciativa, Eiopa busca reforzar la coherencia supervisora en Europa y facilitar un entorno más eficiente para las aseguradoras de menor tamaño. El documento facilita criterios para determinar qué entidades pueden acogerse al nuevo enfoque proporcional y cómo calcular los indicadores de riesgo aplicables.

item.title: Un 73% de los trabajadores demanda un bienestar personalizado en las empresas
item.description: Las empresas han incrementado un 5% su oferta de servicios de bienestar en 2026 respecto al año anterior, consolidando su peso dentro de la estrategia corporativa. Así lo revela el informe de Savia ‘Radiografía del Bienestar en la Empresa’ , que confirma que la media se sitúa en 2,12 soluciones por empleado y el 72% de los trabajadores que recibe estos beneficios afirma que la compañía asume el coste total. El estudio, elaborado a partir de 1.000 encuestas realizadas en febrero de 2026 mediante metodología CAWI, señala que el acceso a estos servicios sigue siendo limitado: solo el 32% de los empleados declara recibir algún tipo de prestación de bienestar.

item.title: Ford Recalling 420,000 Vehicles in US Over Windshield Wiper Failure
item.description: over a failure of the windshield wiper, the U.S. Ford Motor is recalling 422,613 vehicles in the U.S. Related: Ford Recalling 254,000 SUVs in US Due to Software Issues Related: Top 20 Vehicles Sold in United States in Q1 2026 (Reporting by Shah in Bengaluru; Editing by Sherry Jacob-Phillips and Mrigank Dhaniwala) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: US Truck Rates at Highest Since 2022 Add to Inflation Pressures
item.description: Haulers have responded by raising the weekly per-mile fuel surcharge paid by shippers to its highest since 2022, according to Truckstop.com data. Those surcharges come on top of a months-long increase in truck-equipment rates, which are charged to move goods around the country and are higher in part due to trucker shortages. Trucking operators saw diesel prices spike by almost 50% since the start of the U.S.-Israel war against Iran at the end of February.

item.title: Deere Settles Right-to-Repair Suit With $99M Fund, Repair Commitments
item.description: agriculture equipment maker Deere on Monday agreed to pay $99 million into a settlement fund for farms and farmers that are part of a class action over costs and access to repairs. over so-called right-to-repair practices, with regulators and plaintiffs arguing that some manufacturers limit competition by controlling access to repair tools and software. The settlement fund covers eligible plaintiffs who paid Deere’s authorized dealers for repairs to large agricultural equipment from January 2018, according to a document filed on Monday in the federal court in Chicago, Illinois.

item.title: Minhyuk Jung to head up Aon’s South Korea desk
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Specialist risk adviser Continuum forms strategic partnership with Hong Kong-based broker Freed Capital
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zurich hires Cheryn Wong as senior casualty underwriter
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions appoints Jose Maria Lopez as Marine Product Coordinator
item.description: Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions, the reinsurance division of global broker Aon, has announced the appointment of Jose Maria Lopez as Marine Product Coordinator for EMEA Facultative Reinsurance. Lopez joined Aon in October 2014 and has since held a number of senior marine leadership roles, including Marine Associate Manager and, most recently, Marine Regional Practice Leader within Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions since 2018. This role is in addition to Lopez’s current responsibilities leading the Latin America Marine team.

item.title: Pen Underwriting agrees long-term partnership to support non-standard property business
item.description: Andrew Booth, Managing Director of Property & Personal at Pen Underwriting, commented: “We are absolutely delighted to be expanding our decades-long capacity collaboration with Zurich into non-standard household risks and excited to be joining forces with Hiscox in its first strategic partnership with Pen. Pen Underwriting, a multi-class and multi-territory managing general agent (MGA), has entered into a multi-year strategic arrangement to support its non-standard household insurance portfolio across the UK, Channel Islands, and Isle of Man. “Their collective backing, and trust in Pen’s proven expertise in underwriting, means we have significant scope to grow our already large portfolio and meet the rising demand for specialist property insurance as well as from customers with standard homes but specialist needs.

item.title: Odd Mistereggen joins Gallagher Re as Fac broker, EMEA
item.description: Global reinsurance broker Gallagher Re has announced the appointment of Odd Kåre Engh Mistereggen as Facultative Broker, EMEA. Joining the company from rival broker Howden Re, Mistereggen brings a strong analytical approach to complex risk management, drawing on a robust background in project management, retail broking, and reinsurance. Specialising in P&C risk transfer, captives, and facultative reinsurance, Mistereggen has deep experience in designing bespoke solutions for client exposures across areas including PDBI, CAR/EAR, and casualty.

item.title: Viewpoint: The Missing Link in Commercial? No Paper Trail is Costing Insurers
item.description: For commercial property insurers, that missing history is not an abstraction, it is a direct source of claims uncertainty, underwriting exposure, and loss adjustment cost. Consider the moment a major commercial building changes hands. The volume of technical data produced on a contemporary commercial project would have been unimaginable a generation ago.

item.title: Truck Rates at Highest Since 2022 Add to Inflation Pressures
item.description: Haulers have responded by raising the weekly per-mile fuel surcharge paid by shippers to its highest since 2022, according to Truckstop.com data. Those surcharges come on top of a months-long increase in truck-equipment rates, which are charged to move goods around the country and are higher in part due to trucker shortages. Trucking operators saw diesel prices spike by almost 50% since the start of the US-Israel war against Iran at the end of February.

item.title: Conflicto en Medio Oriente: ¿Puede afectar la seguridad digital de bancos de América Latina?
item.description: La banca latinoamericana podría estar expuesta a riesgos que no controla, debido a su dependencia de proveedores tecnológicos globales, que la conecta con conflictos como el de Medio Oriente. Aun así, expertos en seguridad discrepan sobre si ese riesgo es real o todavía remoto. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: Majesco Launches Spring ’26 Release with AI, Workflow Enhancements
item.description: Majesco’s Spring ’26 Release reflects the company’s vision for the future of insurance software; intelligent, embedded, operationally relevant, and built to create measurable business impact for today’s insurance carriers.” For property/casualty insurers, the release includes enhancements to automate high-volume workflows, integrate real-time analytics and improve underwriting and pricing processes. Majesco (Morristown, N.J.) has launched its Spring ’26 Release, introducing new capabilities designed to streamline workflows, enhance automation and expand AI-driven functionality across its insurance platform. The release focuses on reducing operational friction by integrating task management, analytics and AI into core insurance processes, enabling insurers to manage policy, billing and claims workflows within a Manish Shah, President and Chief Product Officer, Majesco.

item.title: Origami Risk Expands Platform with AI, Workflow Automation Tools
item.description: Origami Risk (Chicago) has launched new platform solutions and enhancements designed to automate workflows and deliver real-time insights across claims, policy administration and financial operations. Origami Risk has also introduced a Book of Business Transfer solution that allows insurers and brokers to move large volumes of policies through a governed bulk process, reducing the need for manual reassignment and minimizing errors and compliance risk. All new capabilities are available to Origami Risk customers.

item.title: Cleared for takeoff: war rates remain stable as airlines seek new routes
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: California AG Opposing Oil Pipeline Special Permit to Waive Safety Regulation
item.description: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration’s consideration of Sable Offshore Corp.’s request for a special permit to waive a safety regulation and transport oil through Lines CA-324 and CA-325 in Santa Barbara and Kern counties. California Attorney General Rob Bonta is opposing efforts to wave safety regulations for the Sable oil pipeline. PHSMA is trying to assert federal jurisdiction over the onshore lines by reclassifying them as “interstate,” issuing Sable an emergency permit waiving compliance its safety regulation requiring evaluation and remediation of pipeline corrosion.

item.title: Two Firefighters Killed in Collision During Search for Missing Woman
item.description: Two firefighters traveling in a utility vehicle along a Pennsylvania road during a search for a missing woman were killed in a head-on crash with a car, officials said. No further details about the arrest or the search for the missing woman were immediately available Sunday. Autopsies on the firefighters, both residents of Fleetwood, were scheduled for Monday.

item.title: Why Insurance Needs Specialized AI: The Vertical AI Difference
item.description: From underwriting to policy renewals, and even marketing content creation, industry-specific Vertical AI is touching many areas of an insurance company’s business. How Vertical AI Empowers Insurance Agencies Now here’s where Vertical AI fits in. Investing, Growing, and Succeeding with Specialized AI The future of insurance is here.

item.title: Aon says record reinsurance capital is fueling insurer growth ambitions
item.description: Aon says reinsurance buyers entered the April 1, 2026 renewal with unusually strong leverage, supported by a record $785 billion in global reinsurance capital. Aon frames it as a growth tool, with buyers using favorable conditions to lower costs, improve capital efficiency, and expand into new business lines, geographies, and risk categories. According to the firm, that capital strength helped insurers buy more protection, secure broader structures, and push through double digit rate reductions in several markets, particularly across Asia Pacific.

item.title: Licencias bancarias en México: más que regulación, una apuesta estratégica
item.description: Tras años de operar bajo licencias que les permitieron ofrecer servicios como créditos o ahorros, ahora varias fintechs en México buscan dar el salto hacia la banca múltiple, un cambio con el que podrán operar con un marco regulatorio más amplio y competir de frente con la banca tradicional. “Vamos a llegar al último rincón de México, eso no lo duden. Espero tener todo eso montado este año y, ahora sí, convertirnos en el banco favorito de todo México”, asegura Juan Guerra, director ejecutivo de Revolut México.

item.title: Google Adds Mental Health Tools to Gemini Chatbot After Lawsuit
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google plans to introduce new mental health support features for its Gemini chatbot as the company and rivals, like OpenAI, have faced several lawsuits accusing their artificial intelligence tools of leading to harm. Gemini will add an interface directing chatbot users to a support hotline when the conversation indicates “a potential crisis related to suicide or self-harm,” Google said in a blog post on Tuesday. In March, the family of a deceased 36-year-old man in Florida sued Google, claiming that his use of Gemini culminated in a “four-day descent into violent missions and coached suicide.” At the time, Google said the chatbot referred the man to a crisis hotline many times but promised to improve the tool’s safeguards.

item.title: Russia-Linked Hackers Hijack Routers to Steal Passwords, UK Says
item.description: Russian government-linked hackers are compromising popular internet routers to steal passwords for email accounts and other online services, the U.K.’s National Cyber Security Centre has warned. The hackers, a group known as APT28, are associated with Russia’s GRU military intelligence agency, according to research published Tuesday by the U.K. British cyber officials said they have observed the alleged Russian intruders targeting routers manufactured by companies such as MikroTik and TP-Link.

item.title: Police Chases Lead to 8 Deaths Across the U.S. in Less Than a Week
item.description: A series of police pursuits has led to at least eight deaths around the country in less than a week amid ongoing calls from some law enforcement experts to curb risky high-speed car chases. And in California, three people were killed in vehicle crashes during police pursuits in separate incidents last week. The deadly incidents are among the hundreds of fatalities that occur during police chases each year.

item.title: Stanley Tumbler Maker Prevails in Lawsuit Over Lead Scare
item.description: A federal judge dismissed a proposed class-action lawsuit accusing the maker of Stanley tumblers of concealing that the popular water bottles contained lead. Litigation over the tumblers began after reports of possible lead contamination went viral on social media in early 2024. But in a 41-page decision, Lin said the plaintiffs did not show the use of lead in Stanley tumblers would be material to reasonable consumers.

item.title: Devastating Hawaii Kona Storms Cause Estimated $1B in Losses
item.description: The double Kona storm patterns caused Honolulu to break a 75-year-old 24-hour record, with rainfall totaling nearly 50 inches on Maui and 39 inches on Hawaii. FEMA estimated that more than 400,000 properties statewide don’t carry flood insurance, increasing the likelihood of uninsured losses for homeowners, small businesses, and the agricultural sector. Hawaii’s multi-day storm event last month resulted in swaths of heavy rain totaling up to 25 inches in some areas, according to analysis by Marsh’s Guy Carpenter.

item.title: Florida Retirement System Pension grows ILS allocation to 1% of fund, around $2.23bn
item.description: The Florida Retirement System Pension Plan, which is administered by the Florida State Board of Administration, saw its allocation to insurance-linked securities funds and reinsurance strategies rising to make up 1% of total pension fund assets by the end of 2025, which equated to around $2.23 billion invested. By 2023, the Florida Retirement System pension fund allocation to ILS investments reached the $1 billion dollar mark, with the State Board of Administration developing a plan to capitalise on higher rates and returns possible in a harder reinsurance market to as much as double it . Those allocations are made on behalf of the giant Florida Retirement System Pension Plan, which reached total fund assets of over $222.5 billion by the end of 2025.

item.title: Court of Appeals weighs in on Vesttoo LOC reinsurance broker responsibilities
item.description: The United States Court of Appeals has weighed in on a lawsuit regarding the Vesttoo fraud and the fake letters of credit (LOC) that underpinned reinsurance deals linked to the company, with a finding that could have some ramifications for brokers. The appeal in question came under the lawsuit filed by Porch Group against broker Gallagher Re, regarding the Vesttoo reinsurance letter of credit (LOC) collateral fraud. Porch claims that, “Gallagher incorrectly represented the collateral letter as a letter of credit, ignored red flags throughout the transaction process, assured HOA that the collateral was valid, and assured HOA it could “safely” allow Vesttoo’s $25 million withdrawal from the reinsurance account,” the appeal court ruling states (HOA being Homeowners of America, a Porch insurer subsidiary).
